title: Add a note for exposing ports while using CNI.
body:

action required

Pull request is about adding a note to docs about dealing with port exposing while using CNI networking.
Additional changes might be needed for fitting into the docs page formatting style.

It has been a while since I used CNI with ipv6 but I do not recall ever needing to do this. Also I do not believe that there is a difference between CNI and netavark here, we use the same iptables rules.

It is not clear what you did and why this fixes it? Do you run as root or rootless? Does your cni network has ipv6 enabled?

CNI has IPv6 enabled + I'm running it as root.

I though, commits says enough, but I added a note in the docs about the CNI / IPv6 problem while exposing ports.

``` $ sudo podman --network-backend cni network create t1 --ipv6 t1 $ sudo podman --network-backend cni run -d -p 80:80 --network t1 nginx 892c1edf81a54e697e694f32c31617b9e5477ed65ebe9984f22885be63dd475e $ ip a 1: lo: mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000 link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00 inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever inet6 ::1/128 scope host valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever 2: enp44s0u1: mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000 link/ether 3c:18:a0:57:fc:9a brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff inet 192.168.1.134/24 brd 192.168.1.255 scope global dynamic noprefixroute enp44s0u1 valid_lft 39582sec preferred_lft 39582sec inet6 fe80::7222:5dd3:d486:306d/64 scope link noprefixroute valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever 5: cni-podman1: mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP group default qlen 1000 link/ether b2:6d:1e:51:12:9c brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff inet 10.89.0.1/24 brd 10.89.0.255 scope global cni-podman1 valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever inet6 fdf5:9337:882f:4464::1/64 scope global valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever inet6 fe80::b06d:1eff:fe51:129c/64 scope link valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever 8: veth9d191eef@if2: mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue master cni-podman1 state UP group default link/ether 72:d4:3f:26:32:f0 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff link-netns netns-89feb680-c8a9-dab9-f72b-e6cb5881b98e inet6 fe80::70d4:3fff:fe26:32f0/64 scope link valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever $ curl http://[fdf5:9337:882f:4464::1] Welcome to nginx!

$ sudo ip6tables -t nat -nvL Chain PREROUTING (policy ACCEPT 2 packets, 265 bytes) pkts bytes target prot opt in out source destination 1 122 CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT all * * ::/0 ::/0 ADDRTYPE match dst-type LOCAL Chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT 2 packets, 265 bytes) pkts bytes target prot opt in out source destination Chain OUTPUT (policy ACCEPT 2 packets, 342 bytes) pkts bytes target prot opt in out source destination 1 80 CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT all * * ::/0 ::/0 ADDRTYPE match dst-type LOCAL Chain POSTROUTING (policy ACCEPT 2 packets, 342 bytes) pkts bytes target prot opt in out source destination 3 422 CNI-HOSTPORT-MASQ all * * ::/0 ::/0 /* CNI portfwd requiring masquerade */ 295 25943 LIBVIRT_PRT all * * ::/0 ::/0 0 0 CNI-6d98b454aa8b1a1bb67f327d all * * fdf5:9337:882f:4464::4 ::/0 /* name: "t1" id: "892c1edf81a54e697e694f32c31617b9e5477ed65ebe9984f22885be63dd475e" */ Chain CNI-6d98b454aa8b1a1bb67f327d (1 references) pkts bytes target prot opt in out source destination 0 0 ACCEPT all * * ::/0 fdf5:9337:882f:4464::/64 /* name: "t1" id: "892c1edf81a54e697e694f32c31617b9e5477ed65ebe9984f22885be63dd475e" */ 0 0 MASQUERADE all * * ::/0 !ff00::/8 /* name: "t1" id: "892c1edf81a54e697e694f32c31617b9e5477ed65ebe9984f22885be63dd475e" */ Chain CNI-DN-6d98b454aa8b1a1bb67f3 (1 references) pkts bytes target prot opt in out source destination 1 80 CNI-HOSTPORT-SETMARK tcp * * fdf5:9337:882f:4464::/64 ::/0 tcp dpt:80 1 80 DNAT tcp * * ::/0 ::/0 tcp dpt:80 to:[fdf5:9337:882f:4464::4]:80 ... ``` This is how I tested it, it works just fine without adding `[::]` to the port forward. Please share a exact reproducer if it does not work for you.

Seems like, it works as you said.

But for some reason it doesn't want with exposing ports onto IPv6 network.
Like, exposing ports works with `-p [::]:<hostPort>:<containerPort>`.

> But for some reason it doesn't want with tunneling onto IPv6 network.

I have no idea what this means? Did you try if netavark and/or rootless behaves differently?

In general I do not like to merge such a doc change because it should not be needed and if it doesn't work I rather just fix the code to make it work.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Set `network_backend` to `"cni"` in `/etc/containers/containers.conf`.
2. Set `cni_plugin_dirs`:
```
cni_plugin_dirs = [
  "/usr/local/libexec/cni",
  "/usr/libexec/cni",
  "/usr/local/lib/cni",
  "/usr/lib/cni",
  "/opt/cni/bin",
]
```
3. Set `enable_ipv6` to `true`.
4. Run `podman run -it -p 8080:80 linuxserver/nginx`
5. In a second terminal run `nmap -6 ::1 -p 8080`.
6. Try again with `podman run -it -p 8080:80 -p [::]:8080:80 linuxserver/nginx`.
7. Run `nmap -6 ::1 -p 8080` again.

> Steps to reproduce:
> 
>     1. Set `network_backend` to `"cni"` in `/etc/containers/containers.conf`.
> 
>     2. Set `cni_plugin_dirs`:
> 
> 
> ```
> cni_plugin_dirs = [
>   "/usr/local/libexec/cni",
>   "/usr/libexec/cni",
>   "/usr/local/lib/cni",
>   "/usr/lib/cni",
>   "/opt/cni/bin",
> ]
> ```
> 
>     3. Set `enable_ipv6` to `true`.
> 
>     4. Run `podman run -it -p 8080:80 linuxserver/nginx`
> 
>     5. In a second terminal run `nmap -6 ::1 -p 8080`.
> 
>     6. Try again with `podman run -it -p 8080:80 -p [::]:8080:80 linuxserver/nginx`.
> 
>     7. Run `nmap -6 ::1 -p 8080` again.

Note that port forwarding via `::1` is not supported when you run as root via cni/netavark. The kernel does not support DNAT on `::1` so we cannot add port forward rules. You should check for actual connection traffic not just nmap to see if it is working. Podman will always bind the addresses to make sure no other process is trying to use them. I think what you are seeing here is that we only bind `0.0.0.0` by default and not `::`, this is likely something that needs to be fixed and not the docs.

> I think what you are seeing here is that we only bind `0.0.0.0` by default and not `::`, this is likely something that needs to be fixed and not the docs.

I agree. So close the PR?
Lets close it, could you create a new issue with that information so we/I do not forget it ?
